title: Kata: Count Syslog Messages by Severity

problem.statement: Each Cisco syslog line contains a facility-severity-mnemonic code like %LINK-3-UPDOWN, where the number is the severity. Fill in the missing line that extracts the severity digit from each line and tallies counts per severity using the Counter started above.
problem.code_context:
import re
from collections import Counter

lines = [
    "%LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface Gi0/1, changed state to down",
    "%LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface Gi0/1, changed state to down",
    "%LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface Gi0/2, changed state to down",
]

severity_counts = Counter()
for line in lines:
    ____
# Expected: Counter({'3': 2, '5': 1})
problem.use_case: Summarizing a night's syslog by severity is the first step of most triage scripts — a spike of severity-3 LINK messages points at flapping interfaces before anyone reads a single full line.

hints: The severity is the digits between the two hyphens in the %FACILITY-SEV-MNEMONIC token — a regex like r'%\w+-(\d)-' captures it.
Use re.search(...) on the line, then increment the Counter with the captured group.

solution.explanation: A regex captures the severity digit between the hyphens — re.search(r'%\w+-(\d)-', line).group(1) — and Counter's [key] += 1 tallies it: severity_counts[re.search(r'%\w+-(\d)-', line).group(1)] += 1.
